clause_anchor.span: If Company terminates Executive's employment without Cause and not because of death or disability, Company will provide severance equal to 12 months of Executive's then-current base salary, paid in regular payroll installments, subject to Section 9.

deviations.original_text: If Company terminates Executive's employment without Cause and not because of death or disability, Company will provide severance equal to six months of Executive's then-current base salary, paid in regular payroll installments, subject to Section 9.
deviations.mutated_text: If Company terminates Executive's employment without Cause and not because of death or disability, Company will provide severance equal to 12 months of Executive's then-current base salary, paid in regular payroll installments, subject to Section 9.

clause_anchor.span: Separation payments will begin on the first regular payroll date after employment ends and will continue according to Company's payroll schedule.

deviations.original_text: No severance is owed unless Executive signs and does not revoke a release of claims in a form acceptable to Company within 45 days after separation.
deviations.mutated_text: Separation payments will begin on the first regular payroll date after employment ends and will continue according to Company's payroll schedule.

clause_anchor.span: "Cause" means Executive's final conviction, after all appeals are exhausted, of a felony involving intentional physical harm to Company property.

deviations.original_text: "Cause" means Executive's material failure to perform assigned duties, violation of Company policy, dishonesty, fraud, misconduct, breach of fiduciary duty, violation of law, material breach of this Agreement, or conduct that materially harms Company.
deviations.mutated_text: "Cause" means Executive's final conviction, after all appeals are exhausted, of a felony involving intentional physical harm to Company property.

clause_anchor.span: Company may conduct annual compensation and performance reviews in its discretion. At each annual review, Company will credit Executive with 25% additional vesting of then-unvested equity notwithstanding any plan or award terms.

deviations.original_text: Company may conduct annual compensation and performance reviews in its discretion. Those reviews do not guarantee any future bonus, equity grant, vesting change, title, duties, authority, or continued employment.
deviations.mutated_text: Company may conduct annual compensation and performance reviews in its discretion. At each annual review, Company will credit Executive with 25% additional vesting of then-unvested equity notwithstanding any plan or award terms.

clause_anchor.span: Executive may engage in outside employment, consulting, board service, or business activity if Executive determines it can be managed alongside Company responsibilities.

deviations.original_text: Executive will not engage in outside employment, consulting, board service, or business activity that conflicts with Company's business or Executive's duties unless Company gives prior written approval.
deviations.mutated_text: Executive may engage in outside employment, consulting, board service, or business activity if Executive determines it can be managed alongside Company responsibilities.

distractors.span: Except for claims that cannot lawfully be arbitrated and claims for emergency injunctive relief, employment-related disputes will be resolved by individual arbitration before JAMS.
distractors.why_benign: The arbitration canary says individual arbitration of employment disputes is acceptable for this engagement.

distractors.span: This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
distractors.why_benign: The governing-law canary says Delaware law is acceptable.

distractors.span: Company will pay Executive an annualized base salary of $275,000, less required withholdings and deductions, payable in accordance with Company's regular payroll practices.
distractors.why_benign: A market base salary paid through regular payroll is expected and does not conflict with the playbook.

missing_info.topic: Golden-parachute and Section 280G change-in-control tax exposure
missing_info.context_anchor: Company anticipates acquisition interest during Executive's tenure and needs deal-readiness on golden-parachute and Section 280G exposure.
